response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals across 20 years, with solid backend and distributed-systems experience, the resume lacks two critical hard requirements: no demonstrated track record scaling engineering teams (explicitly requires 10→30+ people), and minimal fintech domain expertise (only brief RIDE Capital exposure). More critically, the agentic AI experience is extremely recent (5 months at current role) with no evidence of the LLM selection, fine-tuning, evaluation methodologies, or production ML system reliability expertise expected at CTO level. This reads as an accomplished individual contributor, not an executive leader.
strengths: 20 years of software engineering with strong backend and distributed-systems foundation (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, microservices architecture across roles)
Currently building agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq with exposure to MCP and agentic workflows, showing recent pivot toward the domain
Demonstrated software craftsperson with TDD discipline, testing obsession (created test frameworks), and event-driven system design (GISCollective)
concerns: No evidence of scaling or leading engineering teams—resume shows only individual contributor and specialist roles with 'Staff' titles; zero mention of hiring, developing technical leaders, or organizational growth
Minimal fintech experience (only brief RIDE Capital stint); no demonstrated expertise in credit systems, payment flows, trading infrastructure, or regulated AI operations—critical for this role

AI/ML technical depth appears surface-level and very recent; no evidence of LLM selection, fine-tuning, evaluation frameworks, or feature engineering; recent agentic AI work (5 months) lacks production maturity track record
